feedback.text: Digital purchases need to be treated properly. The increase in shady definitions of ownership and licensing is a worrying sign when it comes to digital purchases. The developers/sellers can recall or discontinue the products as they please. Like for example, if you do not log in for a year they will remove your account and every purchase associated with it. Ownership needs to become a thing again, and it's only fair for the consumers to know what to expect from a digital purchase. If it's time-limited, it needs to be stated clearly. If support end, an end of life solution should be introduced to preserve the content. It should also only be up to the store/seller to determine what content they want to provide. Recently, we saw lots of content being removed from Steam and Itch.io, due to the payment providers. I believe this an overreach of power and an abuse of a monopolistic position on the market. In this case the target was certain types of games, but other games got caught in the crossfire, and I believe these situations sets a dangerous precedent. The law should be the foundation for what can be sold, not the personal beliefs of a few activists. If these group's personal beliefs are held above the law for what can be sold, then what will be banned next?
